commit.message:
fix(ci): use simple glob for test discovery on Linux

The test script used 'test/**/*.test.ts' which requires bash globstar
(shopt -s globstar) to expand. GitHub Actions runners (ubuntu-latest)
don't enable globstar by default, so the glob was passed literally to
Node and failed with 'Could not find'. Changed to 'test/*.test.ts'
since all test files are directly in the test/ directory.

This fixes the CI/CD pipeline that has been broken for all commits,
preventing any fixes from being deployed to Azure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Python input() — fully interactive programs now work in the runner

The OSError [Errno 29] I/O error happened because Pyodide has no stdin.
Fix: override builtins.input() in the Pyodide iframe with an async version
that messages the parent and pauses Python execution until the user responds.

How it works:
- Python calls input('Hit or Stand? ')
- Pyodide sends input_request to the React panel
- Panel flushes the stdout accumulated since last input (so you see the
  game state before the prompt) then shows an input text field
- User types and presses Enter (or clicks the send button)
- Response is sent back to the iframe, Python resumes with the value
- Repeat for every subsequent input() call
- When execution ends the remaining stdout + plots are shown

Blackjack, text adventures, guessing games, any input()-driven program
will now run interactively end-to-end.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix infinite cron trigger fires during DST fall-back transition (#2865)

* Initial plan

* Fix infinite trigger fires during DST fall-back transition

When computing next fire time in CronExpression.GetTimeAfter, ambiguous
local times during DST fall-back transitions were assigned the DST
(summer) UTC offset, resulting in a UTC time that was before the input.
This caused triggers to always compute a next fire time in the past,
leading to infinite refires.

Fix: after computing the UTC result, if it falls before afterTimeUtc
and the local time is ambiguous, use the standard (non-DST) offset
instead — the minimum of the two ambiguous offsets — which corresponds
to the second (post-fallback) occurrence of the ambiguous local time.

Adds regression test ShouldNotFireInfinitelyAfterDstFallBackTransition
reproducing the Amsterdam/Europe scenario from the issue report.

fix: resolve code scanning alerts — URL sanitization, clear-text logging, dependency pinning (#102)

- Fix py/incomplete-url-substring-sanitization (2 alerts): Use urlparse for
  proper domain validation instead of substring check in test assertions
- Fix py/clear-text-logging-sensitive-data (10 alerts): Replace _redact()
  with hash-based redaction using SHA-256 digests to break taint chain while
  preserving correlation capability across 4 example files
- Fix PinnedDependenciesID in CI workflows (8 alerts): Pin ruff, pytest,
  pytest-asyncio, safety, build, pyyaml to version ranges in ci.yml,
  publish.yml, and policy-validation.yml
- Fix PinnedDependenciesID in Dockerfiles (20+ alerts): Add upper-bound
  version constraints to all requirements.txt files used by Dockerfiles
- Fix PinnedDependenciesID in shell scripts (4 alerts): Pin package versions
  in quickstart.sh, build_and_publish.sh, gh-agent-os, run-demo.sh

32 files changed, 47 alerts addressed.

commit.message:
feat: standardize API error responses with RFC 7807 Problem Details

Closes #1265

New infrastructure:
- ApiErrorWriter.cs: centralized error response writer using Utf8JsonWriter
  directly to response body (zero intermediate allocations)
- ApiError struct with RFC 7807 fields: type, title, status, detail, errorId
- FieldError struct for validation errors array
- ApiErrorTypes constants for well-known error type URIs
- Factory methods: BadRequest, Unauthorized, Forbidden, NotFound,
  RateLimited, UnsupportedMediaType, Conflict, InternalError, ServiceUnavailable

Content type: application/problem+json (RFC 7807)

Centralized error handling:
- BareMetalWebServer catch block: JSON errors now use ApiErrorWriter
- RenderForbidden: content-negotiates JSON (API) vs HTML (browser)
- Throttle response: uses ApiErrorWriter for API clients
- All set X-Error-Id header automatically when errorId is present

Migrated endpoints (Program.cs):
- Device code rate limiting (429)
- Content-Type validation (415)
- Missing device_code (400 with field errors)
- Invalid user id (400)

Remaining 100+ ad-hoc error sites can be migrated incrementally
using the same ApiErrorWriter factory methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(game): prevent freeze from blocking broadcasts + WebPubSub timeouts

Root cause: emitDistributed() awaited in the tick loop blocked the entire
game simulation when WebPubSub REST calls were slow or failing. The Azure
SDK's default 3-retry policy meant a single failed sendToUser took ~7s,
freezing all game ticks for that duration.

Changes:
- Non-blocking broadcasts: scheduleBroadcast() fires and forgets with a
  latest-wins pattern so the tick loop never blocks on network I/O
- WebPubSub retry disabled: maxRetries=0 since stale snapshots are
  worthless in a real-time game
- 2s abort timeout on sendToUser to cap worst-case latency
- Upgrade webPubSubSku default Free_F1 -> Standard_S1 (Free tier exhausts
  20K msgs/day in ~3 min of gameplay)
- Set apiMinReplicas default 0 -> 1 to prevent cold-start disconnections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace datetime.UTC class-attr usage with UTC alias in OTP and password reset routes (#63)

## Bug

`datetime.UTC` is a **module-level** constant added in Python 3.11. It
is **not** a class attribute of `datetime.datetime`.

All three new auth files import `from datetime import datetime` and then
call `datetime.now(datetime.UTC)`, which tries to access `UTC` as a
class attribute — raising:
```
AttributeError: type object 'datetime.datetime' has no attribute 'UTC'
```

This means **every** call to `POST /auth/login/otp/request`, `POST
/auth/password-reset/request`, and `POST /auth/password-reset/confirm`
returned HTTP 500 at runtime.

## Fix

Import `timezone` from `datetime` and use `datetime.now(timezone.utc)`
in all three files.

## Files changed
- `app/api/routes/authentication/login_otp.py` (2 occurrences)
- `app/api/routes/authentication/password_reset_request.py` (1
occurrence)
- `app/api/routes/authentication/password_reset_confirm.py` (1
occurrence)

Closes #62

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
